inheritance-for-shared-behavior.label: Inheritance for Shared Behavior
inheritance-for-shared-behavior.summary: Use a parent class for behavior that several related classes should share.

try_inheritance_for_shared_behavior_sket.title: Try it yourself
try_inheritance_for_shared_behavior_sket.prompt: Create `models/video.py` as another `LibraryItem` subclass with a `minutes` attribute.

ci-membership-practice.title: Share membership behavior with subclasses
ci-membership-practice.prompt: Complete `PremiumMember` as a subclass of `Member` and keep the inherited greeting.
ci-membership-practice.hint: Read the imports first, then put each responsibility in the file named by that import.

help.concept: In a multi-file project, each file should own one clear responsibility and imports should connect those responsibilities.
help.hint_1: Run from the entry file and follow the first failing import or assertion.
help.hint_2: Keep the full project state working, not just the one line you changed.

ci-membership-practice.starterCode:
from models.premium_member import PremiumMember

member = PremiumMember("Kai", 3)
print(member.greeting())
print(member.bonus_points)

sc-inheritance-for-shared-behavior-desig.title: Check the design choice
sc-inheritance-for-shared-behavior-desig.prompt: Which choice best matches the lesson goal for inheritance for shared behavior?
sc-inheritance-for-shared-behavior-desig.hint: Choose the option that keeps object responsibilities clear.

help.concept: Use a parent class for behavior that several related classes should share.
help.hint_1: Look for the answer that reduces duplication without hiding behavior.
help.hint_2: A good OOP answer usually keeps behavior near the object that owns the data.

options.a: Put every class in main.py so imports are never needed.
options.b: Give each class or service a focused responsibility and import it where needed.
options.c: Avoid methods so every object exposes all of its data directly.
options.d: Copy the same logic into each subclass to avoid shared code.

concept.title: Inheritance for Shared Behavior: the idea
concept.bodyMarkdown: **Inheritance for Shared Behavior** is not just syntax. In this course it is practiced inside small projects where one file owns one clear part of the program. Use a parent class for behavior that several related classes should share.

multifile-shape.title: Inheritance for Shared Behavior: the file shape
multifile-shape.bodyMarkdown: The file layout matters. Models hold object state and behavior, services coordinate groups of objects, tests verify behavior, and `main.py` stays thin enough to read.

graded-habit.title: Inheritance for Shared Behavior: what the grader checks
graded-habit.bodyMarkdown: The grader checks behavior from the running project. A strong answer keeps imports working, keeps all files present, and proves the object behavior through output or assertions.
